patches.find: it is encoded in the Antikythera mechanism (~100 BC) and appears in Babylonian eclipse records predating that. Modern lunar laser ranging (LLR) confirms lunar orbital parameters to millimeter precision.
patches.replace: ancient astronomers tracked the closely related Saros eclipse cycle (~18.03 years, 223 synodic months) — encoded in the Antikythera mechanism (~100 BC) and Babylonian eclipse records — which differs from the nodal regression by ~0.58 years; the 18.613-year value was formally derived from gravitational mechanics by Clairaut (1749). Modern lunar laser ranging (LLR) confirms lunar range to millimeter precision, constraining the gravitational-torque orbit model.
patches.rationale: Hole 0 (moderate): Fix Saros/nodal conflation — Antikythera encodes Saros (~18.03 yr) not the nodal regression (18.613 yr). Also fixes hole 3 (minor): LLR phrasing changed from imprecise parameter claim to accurate range claim, matching the detail_verdict_text fix from c4.

patches.find: and no testable deviation at any precision at all
patches.replace: and no testable deviation at any precision
patches.rationale: Hole 1 (minor): Soften the absolute phrasing to avoid the strawman objection that gear-based mechanisms can in principle produce testable deviations (the Antikythera mechanism itself is the existence-proof).

patches.find: This verdict is returned against the dome author’s own stated bar, not a maturity standard we have chosen.

patches.rationale: Hole 2 (minor): Remove redundant second <strong> wrap — two adjacent bold sentences both assert the same author-own-bar framing. Un-bolding the second removes the redundancy while preserving the meaning.

patches.find: pre-empted on the dome author’s own terms.

patches.rationale: Hole 4a (minor): Replace curly apostrophe (U+2019) with straight ASCII apostrophe to match surrounding prose style.
